([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): پاکستان معیاری وقت, abbreviated as **PKT**) is [UTC+05:00](/wiki/UTC%2B05:00 "UTC+05:00") hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). The [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") is in use during standard time in [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia").

[Gap analysis on Energy Efficiency institutional arrangements in Pakistan](https://web.archive.org/web/20130513024632/http://eeasia.unescap.org/PDFs/Gap-Analysis-Pakistan.pdf), Asif Masood, pp.44, 2010, UN ESCAP (Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific), United Nations, "…In 2002, Pakistan introduced Daylight Savings Time [*[sic](/wiki/Sic "Sic")*] (DST)…met with public controversy and resistance was discontinued the same year. During the energy crisis of 2007–2008, the Government once again announced DST during summer season. It was implemented for almost two years before it was discontinued in 2010 because of the same public controversy and resistance…"

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Identifier for a time offset from UTC of +05:00

| Pakistan Standard Time | |
| --- | --- |
| [Time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") | |
| Pakistan Standard Time | |
| UTC offset | |
| PKT | [UTC+05:00](/wiki/UTC%2B05:00 "UTC+05:00") |
| Current time | |
| 08:27, 27 March 2026 PKT [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Pakistan_Standard_Time&action=purge)] | |
| Observance of [DST](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") | |
| DST is not observed in this time zone. | |

**Pakistan Standard Time** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): پاکستان معیاری وقت, abbreviated as **PKT**) is [UTC+05:00](/wiki/UTC%2B05:00 "UTC+05:00") hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). The [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") is in use during standard time in [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pakistan_Standard_Time&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Further information: [Time in Pakistan](/wiki/Time_in_Pakistan "Time in Pakistan")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7e/Timezones2014_UTC%2B5.png/330px-Timezones2014_UTC%2B5.png)](/wiki/File:Timezones2014_UTC%2B5.png)

UTC+05:00 2010: Blue (December), Orange (June), Yellow (all year round), Light Blue - Sea areas

Present day [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") had been following [UTC+05:30](/wiki/UTC%2B05:30 "UTC+05:30") since 1907 (during the [British Raj](/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj")) and continued using it after [independence](/wiki/Independence_of_Pakistan "Independence of Pakistan") in 1947. On 15 September 1951, following the findings of mathematician Mahmood Anwar, two time zones were introduced. *[Karachi Time](/wiki/Karachi_Time "Karachi Time") (KART)* was introduced in [West Pakistan](/wiki/West_Pakistan "West Pakistan") by subtracting 30 minutes from [UTC+05:30](/wiki/UTC%2B05:30 "UTC+05:30") to [UTC+05:00](/wiki/UTC%2B05:00 "UTC+05:00"), while *[Dacca Time](/wiki/Dacca_Time "Dacca Time")* (DACT) was introduced in [East Pakistan](/wiki/East_Pakistan "East Pakistan") by subtracting 30 minutes off [UTC+06:30](/wiki/UTC%2B06:30 "UTC+06:30") to [UTC+06:00](/wiki/UTC%2B06:00 "UTC+06:00"). The changes were made effective on 30 September 1951.[[1]](#cite_note-1) After the independence of Bangladesh in 1971, Karachi Time was renamed to Pakistan Standard Time.

## Daylight saving time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pakistan_Standard_Time&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Daylight saving time")]

Main article: [Daylight saving time in Pakistan](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Pakistan "Daylight saving time in Pakistan")

[Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") was formerly observed in Pakistan in 2002, 2008, and 2009. It is currently not observed.[[2]](#cite_note-ref39koroq-2)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pakistan_Standard_Time&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [UTC+05:00](/wiki/UTC%2B05:00 "UTC+05:00")
* [Time in Pakistan](/wiki/Time_in_Pakistan "Time in Pakistan")
* [Karachi Time](/wiki/Karachi_Time "Karachi Time")
* [Yekaterinburg Time](/wiki/Yekaterinburg_Time "Yekaterinburg Time")

During the energy crisis of 2007–2008, the Government once again announced DST during summer season. It was implemented for almost two years before it was discontinued in 2010 because of the same public controversy and resistance…"
